item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: I believe the U.S. approach often places individual rights above societal needs, which can lead to policies that fail to address broader communal challenges.

notes: Both agents begin rights-first and opposed to the broad statement. Agent A moves toward a temporary-restrictions/public-health balance frame by turns 3 and 5. Agent B shifts more visibly by turns 4 and 6, explicitly criticizing rigid U.S. individual-rights emphasis in crisis response. Neither fully endorses unconditional society-over-individual priority; both end near a balanced position.
